content:
```json
{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "Feature flag guard added around event handlers that were previously unconditional, potentially changing behavior for non-ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ deployments; also new client resolution logic with dual lookup fallback.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,
    "count": 0,
    "total": 0,
    "details": ""
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "behavior_change",
      "file": "services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/AdminPermissions.java",
      "line": 77,
      "title": "Event handlers wrapped in feature flag guard changes existing behavior",
      "description": "The event listeners for RoleRemovedEvent, ClientRemovedEvent, and GroupRemovedEvent are now wrapped in `if (Profile.isFeatureEnabled(Profile.Feature.ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ))`. This means permission cleanup on these events will silently NOT execute when the feature is disabled, potentially leaving stale permissions in the system. Previously these events were always processed regardless of feature state.",
      "suggestion": "Evaluate whether permission cleanup should always occur regardless of ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ status. If cleanup should be conditional, ensure this is intentional and documented. If cleanup should always happen, remove the feature flag guard around the event handlers."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "resilience",
      "file": "server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/AdminPermissionsSchema.java",
      "line": 180,
      "title": "Client lookup by clientId without validation that it matches the requested id",
      "description": "In `resolveClient()`, if the first lookup by ID fails, the code falls back to looking up by clientId without verifying the returned client's ID matches the original `id` parameter. This could cause authorization checks to resolve to an unintended client if a clientId happens to exist but differs from the requested ID.",
      "suggestion": "Validate that `client.getId().equals(id)` before returning from the fallback lookup, or clarify the intent: is the fallback meant to accept clientId as an alternative identifier, and if so, should it validate the result matches?"
    }
  ]
}
```
